behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: (1) trimming to 10 messages, (2) the trimmed history is what is passed to the LLM call boundary. Hypothetical elsewhere: real model token-limit behavior or HTTP failures. Minimum is Integration because the real failure is often at module handoff (history-trimmer -> request builder/client), which a pure single-function test can miss. Integration-brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text; no correctness claim about LLM output quality; no multi-agentic chain requirement. Rejection-gate: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior only says history is trimmed and passed, not that a real external call/model quality must be validated.
behaviors.test_description: Wire history management + LLM request adapter with a fake in-process LLM client; provide >10-message history and assert outbound payload contains only the most recent 10 in correct order.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: response relevance/groundedness quality ('addresses the question' vs 'generic/off-topic'). Hypothetical elsewhere: DB logging, transport retries. This depends on real model output quality; deterministic stubs would always pass and hide true failures. Rejection-gate: rejected Workflow because behavior describes one generation quality check, not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real prompt+history against the production model setup on a fixed evaluation set; score whether each reply answers the asked question (human rubric or adjudicator), and fail if relevance threshold is missed.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: (1) correctness of 'too formal' tone judgment for tier, (2) regeneration trigger. Hypothetical elsewhere: final customer delivery replacement semantics (covered by behavior 5). Tone correctness is model/AI-quality dependent; mocking the checker can hide real misclassification. Integration-brake: no explicit requirement for real OS/network effects. Rejection-gate: rejected Workflow even though regeneration exists, because this behavior asks that detection triggers regen, not that a full multi-step agentic pipeline outcome is validated end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Use real tone-check path on a curated set of overly formal responses for casual-tier customers; assert they are flagged and that regeneration is invoked.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: multi-module persistence contract and timestamp correctness across first response/regenerations. Hypothetical elsewhere: real DB cluster/network outages. Minimum is Integration because failure is at service->repository/schema mapping boundary and can be caught with local DB/filesystem-backed test. Integration-brake: behavior does not require real external network/OS primitives; does not depend on AI output quality; no multi-agentic correctness chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run chat orchestration against a real local test DB (transactional test instance), force a regeneration path, then assert stored records include user message, all generated drafts/final linkage, and accurate timestamp fields/order.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: orchestration/state handoff between tone-check, regeneration result, and customer-facing output selection. Hypothetical elsewhere: whether tone judgment itself is accurate (behavior 3). Minimum is Integration because this is a module interaction bug class (selection/overwrite/emit ordering) and does not require real model quality. Integration-brake: no explicit real OS/network/subprocess requirement; correctness is not about semantic quality of model output; no required multi-agentic quality pipeline. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is about which response is exposed ('second replaces first'), not evaluating LLM content quality.
behaviors.test_description: With deterministic stubbed generation outputs (first='draft A', second='draft B') and tone-check forced true, execute end-to-end in-process flow and assert only 'draft B' is emitted to customer channel and 'draft A' remains internal/log-only.
